children.text: $500,000 until the end of fiscal year 1992 to the Secretary to carry out sections 4711 and 4712(a)(3) of this title;1See References in Text note below.1

$2,000,000 until the end of fiscal year 1992 to the Director and Under Secretary to carry out the studies under sections 4712(a)(1) and 4712(a)(2 of this title;2So in original. Probably should be followed by a closing parenthesis.2

children.label: A
children.text: $2,000,000 for each of fiscal years 1997 and 1998; and

children.label: B
children.text: $3,000,000 for each of fiscal years 1999 through 2002;

children.label: A
children.text: $1,000,000 to the Department of the Interior, to be used by the Director; and

children.label: B
children.text: $1,000,000 to the Secretary; and

children.label: A
children.text: $3,000,000, which shall be made available from funds otherwise authorized to be appropriated if such funds are so authorized, to the Under Secretary to carry out ; andsection 4712(e) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: $500,000 to the Secretary to carry out .section 4712(f) of this title

children.heading: Task Force and aquatic nuisance species program
children.label: b Task Force and aquatic nuisance species program

children.text: $6,000,000 to the Department of the Interior, to be used by the Director to carry out sections 4722 and 4728 of this title;
$1,000,000 to the Department of Commerce, to be used by the Under Secretary to carry out ;section 4722 of this title

$1,625,000, which shall be made available from funds otherwise authorized to be appropriated if such funds are so authorized, to fund aquatic nuisance species prevention and control research under at the Great Lakes Environmental Research Laboratory of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, of which $500,000 shall be made available for grants, to be competitively awarded and subject to peer review, for research relating to Lake Champlain;section 4722(i) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: $2,800,000, which shall be made available from funds otherwise authorized to be appropriated if such funds are so authorized, to fund grants under ;section 1124 of title 33

children.label: B
children.text: $1,200,000 to fund grants to colleges for the benefit of agriculture and the mechanic arts referred to in ; andsection 322 of title 7

children.label: C
children.text: $1,000,000 to fund grants through the Cooperative Fisheries and Wildlife Research Unit Program of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service;
$3,000,000 to the Department of the Army, to be used by the Assistant Secretary to carry out ; andsection 4722(i)(1)(B) of this title

$300,000 to the Department of the Interior, to be used by the Director to fund regional panels and similar entities under , of which $100,000 shall be used to fund activities of the Great Lakes Commission.section 4723 of this title

children.heading: Grants for State management programs
children.label: c Grants for State management programs
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated for each of fiscal years 1997 through 2002 $4,000,000 to the Department of the Interior, to be used by the Director for making grants under , of which $1,500,000 shall be used by the Director, in consultation with the Assistant Secretary, for management of aquatic nuisance vegetation species.section 4724 of this title

children.heading: Intentional introductions policy review
children.label: d Intentional introductions policy review
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated for fiscal year 1991, $500,000 to the Director and the Under Secretary to conduct the intentional introduction policy review under .section 4727 of this title

children.heading: Ballast water management demonstration program
children.label: e Ballast water management demonstration program
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated $2,500,000 to carry out .section 4714 of this title

children.heading: Research
children.label: f Research
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Director $1,000,000 to carry out research on the prevention, monitoring, and control of aquatic nuisance species in Narragansett Bay, Rhode Island. The funds shall be made available for use by the Department of Environmental Management of the State of Rhode Island.
